contexts.context: The dashing <mark>Cranberry</mark>, the cold-blooded Alfred, the gamester...
...it became conspicuous; and Miss Oxmondeley, Mr. <mark>Cranberry</mark>, and Rachel, appeared standing in various attitudes...
"Give me leave to introduce Mr. <mark>Cranberry</mark> to you, papa."
<mark>Cranberry</mark> bowed, scraped, muttered a great deal,...
...a Chinese-temple in these gardens?" asked <mark>Cranberry</mark> with affected eagerness and pleasure.
<mark>Cranberry</mark> had already received his cue from Eliza, and...
..."If I might be allowed to speak," said <mark>Cranberry</mark>, "I think it gives the idea it was...
"Oh, infinitely more ruinous and dilapidated," added <mark>Cranberry</mark>.
...taken wing like it:" a wink to Eliza and <mark>Cranberry</mark> followed this sentence.
...the deception will have the better effect," remarked <mark>Cranberry</mark>.
